channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Hacker News] Write code like a human will maintain it
item.description: Write code like a human will maintain it

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/mattpocock — Skills for Real Engineers. Straight from my .claude directory.
item.description: Developer Matt Pocock shared engineering skills configuration from his .claude directory on GitHub.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] jbeder/yaml-cpp — A YAML parser and emitter in C++
item.description: yaml-cpp is a C++ library for parsing and emitting YAML data format.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] davila7/claude-code-templates — CLI tool for configuring and monitoring Claude Code
item.description: davila7/claude-code-templates is a CLI tool for configuring and monitoring Claude Code.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] tailscale/tailscale — The easiest, most secure way to use WireGuard and 2FA.
item.description: This is not an AI/ML news item—it's about Tailscale, a VPN/networking tool. Please provide an actual AI/ML news item to summarize.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] google-labs-code/stitch-skills — A library of Agent Skills designed to work with the Stitch MCP server. Each skill follows the Agent Skills open standard, for compatibility with coding agents such as Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Cursor.
item.description: Google released stitch-skills, a library of Agent Skills following the open standard for use with Stitch MCP server and compatible coding agents.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] hashicorp/terraform — Terraform enables you to safely and predictably create, change, and improve infrastructure. It is a source-available tool that codifies APIs into declarative configuration files that can be shared amongst team members, treated as code, edited, reviewed, and versioned.
item.description: Terraform is a source-available infrastructure-as-code tool that converts APIs into shareable, versionable declarative configuration files.

item.title: [Hacker News] AI-generated videos to maximally drive a target brain region
item.description: AI-generated videos to maximally drive a target brain region

item.title: [OpenAI] How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
item.description: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] OpenAI launches GPT 5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna, Codex becomes ChatGPT superapp
item.description: [AINews] OpenAI launches GPT 5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna, Codex becomes ChatGPT superapp

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Unveiling Public Opinion: A Study of Sentiment Analysis Using LSTM and Traditional Models
item.description: Study compares machine learning models for Twitter sentiment analysis on Kaggle data, showing LSTM achieves 80% testing accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] From Solvers to Research: Large Language Model-Driven Formal Mathematics at the Research Frontier
item.description: LLMs excel at formal proofs for defined problems but struggle with frontier mathematics; researchers propose shifting toward AI research agents for open-ended exploration.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] DeepSearch-World: Self-Distillation for Deep Search Agents in a Verifiable Environment
item.description: DeepSearch-Evolve uses a verifiable environment with 420K QA tasks to enable web agents to self-improve without external model distillation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] How Do I Know What to Say Next? Barenholtz's Autogenerative Theory as an Enrichment of Harrisean Integrationism
item.description: Autogenerative theory fills gaps in Integrationist linguistics by explaining structural mechanisms underlying language and LLM behavior.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Scalable and Culturally Specific Stereotype Dataset Construction via Human-LLM Collaboration
item.description: New Spanish-language stereotype dataset EspanStereo, built through human-LLM collaboration, identifies cultural biases in language models across Spanish-speaking regions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Debiasing Backfires: Counterintuitive Side Effects of Preprocessing-Based Stereotype Mitigation
item.description: Debiasing methods in NLP reduce targeted stereotypes but cause unintended increases in stereotyping for other demographic groups.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Multi-cluster Boundary Learning Method for Out-of-Scope Intent Detection via MiniLM Embedding
item.description: Multi-cluster boundary learning method using MiniLM embedding achieves state-of-the-art performance for detecting out-of-scope intents with lightweight one-class classification.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Implausible Tokens Get Reinforced: Tail-Aware Credit Calibration for LLM Reinforcement Learning
item.description: TACO addresses positive-credit contamination in LLM reinforcement learning by calibrating credit assignment to suppress reinforcement of rare erroneous tokens.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Reliability Assessment of LALM Audio Judges for Full-Duplex Voice Agents
item.description: Study assesses Gemini models as audio judges for full-duplex voice agents, finding 2.5 Flash achieves human-comparable reliability on most dimensions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Hallucination Self-Play: Bootstrapping Reinforced Detector via Evolved Generator
item.description: Framework uses self-play between detector and generator models to iteratively improve hallucination detection in LLMs with limited labeled data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] From Execution to Education: A Bloom-Aligned Framework for Measuring Educational Control in LLMs
item.description: Researchers introduce a Bloom-aligned framework showing LLMs can increase task difficulty reliably but struggle to lower cognitive demand for learners.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Tool-Making and Self-Evolving LLM Agents in Low-Latency Systems
item.description: Tool-making pipeline compiles repeated LLM agent steps into validated tools before deployment, reducing production latency by 42% and error rates by up to 53%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Can We Trust LLM's Logic? Quantifying Uncertainty, Coherence, and Robustness via a Graph-Based Framework
item.description: New GRAPHEVAL framework detects unfaithful LLM reasoning by analyzing semantic-structural coherence, exposing flaws missed by final-answer agreement metrics.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Structured Pruning of Large Language Models via Power Transformation and Sign-Preserving Score Aggregation with Adaptive Feature Retention
item.description: Structured pruning method for large language models using power transformation and sign-preserving aggregation maintains accuracy while achieving practical inference speedup.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] PLURAL: A Global Dataset for Value Alignment
item.description: PLURAL, a 500,000-item preference dataset from 20 countries, enables LLMs to better reflect diverse global values and reduce Western-centric bias.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] What LLM Forecasters Know but Don't Say: Probing Internal Representations for Calibration and Faithfulness
item.description: Internal representation probes improve LLM forecaster calibration more than chain-of-thought reasoning, which often conceals actual decision logic.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Holographic Neural PCFG for Unsupervised Parsing
item.description: Holographic Neural PCFG uses algebraic relation modeling for unsupervised constituency parsing, achieving state-of-the-art results with 99.94% fewer parameters.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] COBART: Controlled, Optimized, Bidirectional and Auto-Regressive Transformer for Ad Headline Generation
item.description: COBART uses prefix control tokens with BART fine-tuning to generate ad headlines with controllable length and 5.82% CTR improvement over baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] MASTE: A Multi-Agent Pipeline for Zero-Shot Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction
item.description: MASTE decomposes aspect sentiment triplet extraction into sequential multi-agent stages for improved zero-shot performance without labeled data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] COALA: Robust Contextualized Speech-augmented Language Modeling for ASR via Contrastive Regularizer and Biasing Score Estimation
item.description: COALA improves automatic speech recognition for domain-specific entities by enhancing contextual biasing in speech-augmented language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ICDAR 2026 HIPE-OCRepair Competition on LLM-Assisted OCR Post-Correction for Historical Documents
item.description: ICDAR 2026 HIPE-OCRepair competition evaluated LLM systems for correcting OCR errors in historical documents across English, French, and German.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LEXIC: Lightweight Eye-tracking eXtension via Injected Complexity
item.description: LEXIC improves gaze-only reading comprehension models by injecting word-level difficulty signals, achieving up to 2.9 percentage point gains.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SQuaD-SQL: Efficient Text-to-SQL with Small Language Models via LLM-Guided Knowledge Distillation
item.description: SQuaD-SQL enables small language models to achieve near-LLM performance on Text-to-SQL tasks through knowledge distillation and synthetic data generation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Hidden Decoding at Scale: Latent Computation Scaling for Large Language Models
item.description: Hidden Decoding improves LLM performance via latent computation scaling, expanding tokens into multiple streams with efficient attention while maintaining fixed model architecture.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Diarization-Guided Qwen-ASR Adaptation for Multilingual Two-Speaker Conversational Speech
item.description: ASR system combining speaker diarization, synthetic speech fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning achieves 17.97% error rate on multilingual two-speaker conversational speech.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Best-of-$N$ TTS Evaluation is Confounded by ASR Family Alignment
item.description: Best-of-N TTS verifiers show ranking reversals across ASR families; cross-family ensembles improve consistency and reduce word-error rates.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] XALPHA: A Memory-Driven AI Quant Researcher for Hypothesis-to-Code Alpha Discovery
item.description: Researchers introduce XAlpha, a memory-driven AI system automating hypothesis-to-code discovery for trading signals through a multi-brain architecture.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] TypeProbe: Recovering Type Representations from Hidden States of Pre-trained Code Models
item.description: Researchers show pre-trained code models encode type information in hidden states that transfers across Java and Python.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Grounded Event Extraction from SEC 8-K Filings with a Fine-Grained Taxonomy
item.description: LLM system extracts 119 event types from 292,984 SEC 8-K filings using grounded source citations and quality scoring, reaching 96% precision.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Echoes Across Vietnam's Highlands, Delta, and Coast: A Multilingual Corpus for Cham, Khmer, and Tay-Nung
item.description: CKTN corpus and script-aware adaptation improve NLP for Vietnamese minority languages Cham, Khmer, and Tay-Nung.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Towards the Explainability of Temporal Graph Networks via Memory Backtracking and Topological Attribution
item.description: New explainability method for Temporal Graph Networks uses memory backtracking and topological attribution to explain which historical events drive predictions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Who Gets Missed in the Tail? Thresholded Subgroup Underdiagnosis in Long-Tailed Chest X-ray Classification
item.description: Subgroup-aware weighting and threshold selection reduce missed rare disease diagnoses in chest X-ray classification.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LLT: Local Linear Transformer for PDE Operator Learning
item.description: Local Linear Transformer (LLT) improves PDE operator learning with linear attention and local spatial mixing, achieving competitive accuracy and 1.8-2.5x faster training than baseline methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ReCoLoRA: Spectrum-Aware Recursive Consolidation for Continual LLM Fine-Tuning
item.description: ReCoLoRA introduces recursive consolidation of low-rank adapters for continual LLM fine-tuning, preventing task interference in multi-task sequences.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Omni-Sleep: A Sleep Foundation Model via Hierarchical Contrastive Learning of CNS--ANS Dynamic
item.description: Omni-Sleep foundation model uses physiological CNS-ANS hierarchy to improve sleep staging and disease classification across multimodal polysomnography signals.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Uncertainty-gated selection for block-sparse attention
item.description: Value-of-information routing improves block-sparse attention by expanding token selection for queries with small top-k score gaps.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SHIFT: Survival Prediction from Incomplete and Heterogeneous Genomic Data
item.description: SHIFT is a transformer-based survival model that predicts from incomplete genomic data across institutions without test-time imputation, improving cross-cohort generalization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Jet-Long: Efficient Long-Context Extension with Dynamic Bifocal RoPE
item.description: Jet-Long extends LLM context to 128K tokens using dynamic bifocal RoPE without tuning, outperforming existing methods while maintaining short-context fidelity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Architecture Generalization with MetaNCA
item.description: MetaNCA framework learns local rules to self-organize neural network weights across diverse architectures without backpropagation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LiST: Lipschitz Scaling Training for Robust and Calibrated Neural Networks
item.description: Lipschitz Scaling Training iteratively adjusts Lipschitz constants to create neural networks that are simultaneously accurate, robust, and calibrated.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Selective Left-Shift: Turning Test-Time Compute and Difficulty-based Curation into Training Data for Low-Resource Code Generation
item.description: Three-phase pipeline improves code generation for low-resource languages by converting test-time compute into verified synthetic training data for small models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Transdiagnostic Space of Disorder Like Phenotypes in Reinforcement Learning Agents
item.description: Researchers model seven psychological disorders in RL agents using controllable appraisal signals, finding graded dose-response relationships and a 2D affective space organization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Image classification via a quantum-inspired strategy involving a mixture of experts
item.description: Hybrid quantum-classical mixture of experts reduces image classification error by 50% using quantum feature extraction and classical prediction networks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Importance of Encoder Choice:A Tabular-Image Study
item.description: Study evaluates state-of-the-art tabular models as encoders in image-tabular multimodal learning, addressing label requirements in In-Context Learning models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Scalable and Trustworthy Earth Observation Foundation Models
item.description: Earth observation foundation models require domain-specific design accounting for satellite physics and operational constraints; no single model universally performs best.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Trustworthy Machine Learning through the Lens of Combinatorial Optimization: Survey and Research Perspectives
item.description: Survey highlights combinatorial optimization's role in trustworthy ML, offering global guarantees for interpretability, robustness, fairness, privacy, and certifiability.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Unlocking Temporal Generalization in Hamiltonian Video Dynamics Models
item.description: Hamiltonian video models fixed to predict physics at multiple time scales beyond training regime.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Principled Analysis of Deep Reinforcement Learning Evaluation and Design Paradigms
item.description: Paper shows reinforcement learning algorithm performance rankings don't monotonically improve with more data, challenging evaluation paradigms.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Graph-Regularized Deep Learning for EEG-Based Emotion Recognition with Psychologically-Grounded Label Structure
item.description: Graph-regularized framework leveraging emotion topology improves EEG-based emotion recognition across multiple deep learning architectures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A law of robustness for two-layer neural networks with arbitrary weights
item.description: Theory proves two-layer ReLU networks fitting noisy labels must have Lipschitz constants at least √(n/m), independent of weights.
